tokio::spawn(async move {\n            scoped_key_rotation::run_scoped_key_rotator(rotation_state).await;\n        });\n\n        // Start admin session revocation Pub/Sub subscriber (plan §9).\n        // When any pod revokes a session (logout), the session ID is published\n        // to `miroir:admin_session:revoked`. Every pod subscribes and adds the\n        // ID to its in-memory DashMap, ensuring revoked cookies are rejected\n        // across all pods within milliseconds.\n        let revoked_sessions = state.auth.revoked_sessions.clone();\n        let revoked_total = state.auth.admin_session_revoked_total.clone();\n        let redis_url = config.task_store.url.clone();\n        let key_prefix = redis.key_prefix().to_string();\n        tokio::spawn(async move {\n            info!(\"starting admin session revocation subscriber\");\n            if let Err(e) = miroir_core::task_store::RedisTaskStore::subscribe_session_revocations(\n                &redis_url,\n                &key_prefix,\n                move |session_id: String| {\n                    revoked_sessions.insert(session_id, ());\n                    revoked_total.inc();\n                },\n            )\n            .await\n            {\n                error!(error = %e, \"admin session revocation subscriber exited with error\");\n            }\n        });\n    }\n\n    // Build the main app router with UnifiedState\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\"/health\", get(health::get_health))\n        .route(\"/version\", get(version::get_version::<UnifiedState>))\n        .route(\"/stats\", get(indexes::global_stats_handler))\n        .nest(\"/_miroir\", admin::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/indexes\", indexes::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/keys\", keys::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/search\", search::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/settings\", settings::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        .nest(\"/tasks\", tasks::router::<UnifiedState>())\n        // IMPORTANT: Layer order matters! Last layer() call = outermost = runs first.\n        // The middleware stack (from outermost to innermost):\n        // 1. csrf_middleware - runs first\n        // 2. auth_middleware\n        // 3. Extension layers\n        // 4. request_id_middleware - sets X-Request-Id header\n        // 5. telemetry_middleware - reads X-Request-Id, creates tracing span with request_id field\n        //    The span's request_id field propagates to all child log events via with_current_span(true)\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            auth::CsrfState {\n                auth: state.auth.clone(),\n                redis_store: state.redis_store.clone(),\n            },\n            auth::csrf_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            state.auth.clone(),\n            auth::auth_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::Extension(std::sync::Arc::new(state.admin.clone())))\n        .layer(axum::Extension(state.admin.config.clone()))\n        .layer(axum::extract::DefaultBodyLimit::max(\n            config.server.max_body_bytes as usize,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(\n            middleware::request_id_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            TelemetryState {\n                metrics: state.metrics.clone(),\n                pod_id: state.pod_id.clone(),\n            },\n            middleware::telemetry_middleware,\n        ))\n        .with_state(state.clone());\n\n    let main_addr: SocketAddr = format!(\"{}:{}\", config.server.bind, config.server.port)\n        .parse()\n        .map_err(|e| anyhow::anyhow!(\"Invalid bind address: {}\", e))?;\n    let metrics_addr: SocketAddr = SocketAddr::from(([0, 0, 0, 0], 9090));\n\n    info!(\n        main_addr = %main_addr,\n        metrics_addr = %metrics_addr,\n        \"listening\"\n    );\n\n    // Create listeners\n    let main_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(main_addr).await?;\n    let metrics_listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(metrics_addr).await?;\n\n    // Spawn main server with graceful shutdown\n    let main_server = axum::serve(main_listener, app)\n        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n\n    // Spawn metrics server with graceful shutdown\n    let metrics_app = metrics_router().with_state(state.metrics.clone());\n    let metrics_server = axum::serve(metrics_listener, metrics_app)\n        .with_graceful_shutdown(shutdown_signal());\n\n    // Run both servers concurrently\n    let (main_result, metrics_result) = tokio::join!(\n        main_server,\n        metrics_server\n    );\n\n    // Check for errors\n    if let Err(e) = main_result {\n        error!(\"main server error: {}\", e);\n    }\n    if let Err(e) = metrics_result {\n        error!(\"metrics server error: {}\", e);\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n/// Background health checker - promotes nodes to Active when reachable.\n///\n/// On each tick it also updates the Prometheus metrics for node health,\n/// shard coverage, shard distribution, and degraded shard count.\nasync fn run_health_checker(state: admin_endpoints::AppState) {\n    let mut interval = tokio::time::interval(Duration::from_millis(\n        state.config.health.interval_ms,\n    ));\n\n    loop {\n        interval.tick().await;\n\n        let mut topo = state.topology.write().await;\n        let mut all_healthy = true;\n\n        // Collect node IDs to iterate\n        let node_ids: Vec<_> = topo.nodes().map(|n| n.id.clone()).collect();\n\n        for node_id in &node_ids {\n            // Get current node status\n            let current_status = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.status);\n\n            // Skip nodes that are already Active/Healthy\n            if let Some(NodeStatus::Active) | Some(NodeStatus::Healthy) = current_status {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Get node address\n            let node_address = match topo.node(node_id) {\n                Some(n) => n.address.clone(),\n                None => {\n                    all_healthy = false;\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            // Try to reach the node\n            let client = match reqwest::Client::builder()\n                .timeout(Duration::from_millis(state.config.health.timeout_ms))\n                .build()\n            {\n                Ok(c) => c,\n                Err(_) => {\n                    all_healthy = false;\n                    continue;\n                }\n            };\n\n            let url = format!(\"{}/health\", node_address.trim_end_matches('/'));\n            let result = client.get(&url).send().await;\n\n            if result.is_ok() && result.unwrap().status().is_success() {\n                // Node is reachable - promote to Active\n                if let Some(node) = topo.node_mut(node_id) {\n                    let _ = node.transition_to(NodeStatus::Active);\n                    info!(node_id = %node_id, \"node promoted to Active\");\n                }\n            } else {\n                all_healthy = false;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Update node health gauges (§10 node metrics)\n        for node_id in &node_ids {\n            let healthy = topo.node(node_id).map(|n| n.is_healthy()).unwrap_or(false);\n            state.metrics.set_node_healthy(node_id.as_str(), healthy);\n        }\n\n        // Compute and update shard metrics (§10 shard metrics)\n        update_shard_metrics(&topo, &state.metrics);\n\n        // Update task registry size gauge\n        let task_count = state.task_registry.count();\n        state.metrics.set_task_registry_size(task_count as f64);\n\n        // Mark ready once all configured nodes are reachable\n        if all_healthy && !state.config.nodes.is_empty() {\n            state.mark_ready().await;\n        }\n\n        // Update §14.9 resource-pressure metrics\n        update_resource_pressure_metrics(&state.metrics);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Compute shard coverage, degraded count, and per-node shard distribution\n/// from the current topology and update the corresponding Prometheus gauges.\nfn update_shard_metrics(topo: &Topology, metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n    let node_map = topo.node_map();\n    let mut healthy_shards = 0u64;\n    let mut degraded_shards = 0u64;\n\n    // Per-node shard count\n    let mut node_shard_counts: std::collections::HashMap<miroir_core::topology::NodeId, u64> =\n        std::collections::HashMap::new();\n\n    for shard_id in 0..topo.shards {\n        let mut has_healthy_replica = false;\n        for group in topo.groups() {\n            let assigned = miroir_core::router::assign_shard_in_group(\n                shard_id, group.nodes(), topo.rf(),\n            );\n            for node_id in &assigned {\n                let healthy = node_map\n                    .get(node_id)\n                    .map(|n| n.is_healthy())\n                    .unwrap_or(false);\n                if healthy {\n                    has_healthy_replica = true;\n                    *node_shard_counts.entry(node_id.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        if has_healthy_replica {\n            healthy_shards += 1;\n        } else {\n            degraded_shards += 1;\n        }\n    }\n\n    let coverage = if topo.shards > 0 {\n        healthy_shards as f64 / topo.shards as f64\n    } else {\n        1.0\n    };\n    metrics.set_shard_coverage(coverage);\n    metrics.set_degraded_shards(degraded_shards as f64);\n\n    for (node_id, count) in &node_shard_counts {\n        metrics.set_shard_distribution(node_id.as_str(), *count as f64);\n    }\n}\n\n/// Read cgroup v2 memory pressure and update §14.9 resource-pressure gauges.\n///\n/// In Kubernetes each container has its own cgroup; the paths below are the\n/// standard cgroup v2 mount points. If the files don't exist (e.g. local dev\n/// on macOS) the metrics remain at their zero defaults.\nfn update_resource_pressure_metrics(metrics: &middleware::Metrics) {\n    // ── Memory pressure ──\n    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current and memory.max\n    let mem_current = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.current\");\n    let mem_max = read_cgroup_metric(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max\");\n\n    if let (Some(current), Some(max)) = (mem_current, mem_max) {\n        if max > 0 {\n            let ratio = current as f64 / max as f64;\n            let level = if ratio > 0.90 { 2 } else if ratio > 0.75 { 1 } else { 0 };\n            metrics.set_memory_pressure(level);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── CPU throttling ──\n    // cgroup v2: /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat contains throttle_usec\n    if let Ok(contents) = std::fs::read_to_string(\"/sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.stat\") {\n        for line in contents.lines() {\n            if let Some(rest) = line.strip_prefix(\"throttled_usec \") {\n                if let Ok(usec) = rest.trim().parse::<f64>() {\n                    // Report delta — the counter is cumulative, so we report\n                    // the raw value and let Prometheus handle rate().\n                    // For simplicity we set the counter to the absolute value\n                    // (Prometheus counters are monotonic; since this is called\n                    // periodically, we just inc by the new delta).\n                    // Actually, the metric is a Counter, so we can only inc it.\n                    // We'll read the previous throttled value from a thread-local.\n                    // Simpler approach: just report the current throttle time\n                    // as a one-shot increment if non-zero.\n                    metrics.inc_cpu_throttled_seconds(usec / 1_000_000.0);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── Peer pod count and leader status ──\n    // In the current single-pod or HA-proxy model, peer count = configured nodes\n    // that are healthy. Leader is always true for the active pod (no election yet).\n    // These will be refined when peer discovery (§14.3) lands.\n    metrics.set_peer_pod_count(1);\n    metrics.set_leader(true);\n    metrics.set_owned_shards_count(0);\n}\n\n/// Read a single integer value from a cgroup pseudo-file.\nfn read_cgroup_metric(path: &str) -> Option<u64> {\n    std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n        .ok()\n        .and_then(|s| s.trim().parse::<u64>().ok())\n}\n\n/// Graceful shutdown signal handler.\nasync fn shutdown_signal() {\n    let ctrl_c = async {\n        signal::ctrl_c()\n            .await\n            .expect(\"failed to install Ctrl+C handler\");\n    };\n\n    #[cfg(unix)]\n    let terminate = async {\n        signal::unix::signal(signal::unix::SignalKind::terminate())\n            .expect(\"failed to install signal handler\")\n            .recv()\n            .await;\n    };\n\n    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n    let terminate = std::future::pending::<()>;\n\n    tokio::select! {\n        _ = ctrl_c => {},\n        _ = terminate => {},\n    }\n\n    info!(\"shutdown signal received, draining in-flight requests...\");\n\n    // Shutdown OpenTelemetry to flush any pending traces\n    otel::shutdown_otel();\n}\n","numLines":569,"startLine":1,"totalLines":569}}}

{\n19\t    serde_json::from_str(line).ok()\n20\t}\n21\t\n22\t/// Helper: check if a string is a valid 8-char hex request ID (from RequestId::new)\n23\tfn is_valid_request_id(s: &str) -> bool {\n24\t line.to_lowercase();\n30\t    // Check for API key patterns\n31\t    if lower.contains(\"api_key\") || lower.contains(\"apikey\") || lower.contains(\"api-key\") {\n32\t        // Allow if it's just \"api_key_hash\" or similar\n33\t        if !lower.contains(\"hash\") && !lower.contains(\"uid\") {\n34\t            return true;\n35\t        }\n36\t    }\n37\t    // Check for master keys\n38\t    if lower.contains(\"master\") && (lower.contains(\"key\") || lower.contains(\"secret\")) {\n39\t        return true;\n40\t    }\n41\t    // Check for JWT tokens\n42\t    if lower.contains(\"eyj\") && lower.len() > 100 {\n43\t        // JWT base64 pattern\n44\t        return true;\n45\t    }\n46\t    // Check for document content patterns (long text blocks in logs)\n47\t    if line.len() > 5000 {\n55\t            return true;\n56\t        }\n57\t    }\n58\t    false\n59\t}\n60\t\n61\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n62\t// Acceptance Criterion P7.5 Structured JSON logging + request IDs + trace correlation tests.\n//!\n//! Tests for plan §10 structured logging format:\n//! - JSON-per-line parseable by jq\n//! - request_id on every log line (same as X-Request-Id header)\n//! - No PII (API keys, document content, query strings) in logs\n//! - Log volume: 2 INFO entries per search request (middleware + search handler)\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::Request,\n    body::Body,\n    http::StatusCode,\n    Router,\n};\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\n\n/// Helper: parse a JSON log line and extract fields\nfn parse_log_line(line: &str) -> Option<serde_json::Value> {\n    serde_json::from_str(line).ok()\n}\n\n/// Helper: check if a string is a valid 8-char hex request ID (from RequestId::new)\nfn is_valid_request_id(s: &str) -> bool {\n    s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit())\n}\n\n/// Helper: check if a line contains PII patterns\nfn contains_pii(line: &str) -> bool {\n    let lower = line.to_lowercase();\n    // Check for API key patterns\n    if lower.contains(\"api_key\") || lower.contains(\"apikey\") || lower.contains(\"api-key\") {\n        // Allow if it's just \"api_key_hash\" or similar\n        if !lower.contains(\"hash\") && !lower.contains(\"uid\") {\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n    // Check for master keys\n    if lower.contains(\"master\") && (lower.contains(\"key\") || lower.contains(\"secret\")) {\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for JWT tokens\n    if lower.contains(\"eyj\") && lower.len() > 100 {\n        // JWT base64 pattern\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for document content patterns (long text blocks in logs)\n    if line.len() > 5000 {\n        // Very long log line might contain document content\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for raw query strings (both URL-style q= and JSON-style \"q\":)\n    if (lower.contains(\"q=\") || lower.contains(\"\\\"q\\\":\")) && line.len() > 200 {\n        // But not if the value is explicitly redacted\n        if !lower.contains(\"\\\"[redacted]\\\"\") {\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n    false\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance Criterion 1: jq parses every log line\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_json_logs_parseable_by_jq() {\n    // Sample log entry in the expected format\n    let sample_log = r#\"{\n        \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\n        \"level\": \"info\",\n        \"target\": \"miroir.request\",\n        \"message\": \"GET /indexes/products/search 200\",\n        \"request_id\": \"abc123\",\n        \"pod_id\": \"miroir-proxy-7f8d9c6d5-abc12\",\n        \"duration_ms\": 42,\n        \"status\": 200,\n        \"method\": \"GET\",\n        \"path_template\": \"/indexes/{uid}/search\"\n    }\"#;\n\n    let parsed = parse_log_line(sample_log);\n    assert!(parsed.is_some(), \"Sample log should parse as JSON\");\n\n    let log_obj = parsed.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"level\"], \"info\");\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"target\"], \"miroir.request\");\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"request_id\"], \"abc123\");\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"duration_ms\"], 42);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_request_id_format_in_logs() {\n    // Verify request_id appears in JSON logs\n    let sample_log = r#\"{\n        \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\n        \"level\": \"info\",\n        \"target\": \"miroir.request\",\n        \"message\": \"search completed\",\n        \"request_id\": \"a1b2c3d4\",\n        \"pod_id\": \"test-pod\",\n        \"duration_ms\": 42\n    }\"#;\n\n    let parsed = parse_log_line(sample_log).unwrap();\n    let request_id = parsed[\"request_id\"].as_str().unwrap();\n\n    // Request IDs should be 8 hex chars (from RequestId::new())\n    assert_eq!(request_id.len(), 8);\n    assert!(request_id.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()));\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance Criterion 2: request_id traceable across pods\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_request_id_extraction_from_logs() {\n    let logs = vec![\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.request\",\"request_id\":\"abc12345\",\"pod_id\":\"pod-1\",\"message\":\"GET /search 200\"}\"#,\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.001Z\",\"level\":\"debug\",\"target\":\"miroir.node\",\"request_id\":\"abc12345\",\"pod_id\":\"pod-1\",\"node_id\":\"node-1\",\"message\":\"node call started\"}\"#,\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.010Z\",\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.search\",\"request_id\":\"abc12345\",\"pod_id\":\"pod-1\",\"index\":\"products\",\"message\":\"search completed\"}\"#,\n    ];\n\n    // Extract all logs with request_id = \"abc12345\"\n    let target_id = \"abc12345\";\n    let matching_logs: Vec<_> = logs\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|line| line.contains(&format!(\"\\\"request_id\\\":\\\"{}\\\"\", target_id)))\n        .collect();\n\n    assert_eq!(matching_logs.len(), 3, \"Should find all 3 log lines for the request\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance Criterion 3: No PII in logs\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_no_api_keys_in_logs() {\n    // These patterns should NOT appear in logs\n    let pii_patterns = vec![\n        r#\"\"api_key\": \"master_key_12345\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"bearer\": \"Bearer master_key_12345\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"authorization\": \"Bearer master_key_12345\"\"#,\n    ];\n\n    for pattern in pii_patterns {\n        assert!(\n            contains_pii(pattern),\n            \"Pattern should be flagged as PII: {}\",\n            pattern\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Hashed versions are OK\n    let safe_patterns = vec![\n        r#\"\"api_key_hash\": \"a1b2c3d4\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"key_uid\": \"12345\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"tenant_id\": \"tenant-123\"\"#,\n    ];\n\n    for pattern in safe_patterns {\n        assert!(\n            !contains_pii(pattern),\n            \"Hashed pattern should NOT be flagged as PII: {}\",\n            pattern\n        );\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_no_query_strings_in_logs() {\n    // Raw Meilisearch query content should not be logged\n    // The contains_pii function checks for long q= patterns (user queries)\n    // Line must be >200 chars total and contain \"q=\" to trigger PII detection\n    let padding = \"x\".repeat(200);\n    let bad_log = format!(r#\"{{\"message\": \"processing search\", \"q\": \"SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = 'test@example.com'\", \"padding\": \"{}\"}}\"#, padding);\n    assert!(contains_pii(&bad_log), \"Long query string should be flagged as PII\");\n\n    // Redacted queries are OK\n    let good_log = r#\"{\"message\": \"processing search\", \"query\": \"[redacted]\", \"q\": \"[redacted]\"}\"#;\n    assert!(!contains_pii(good_log), \"Redacted query should NOT be flagged as PII\");\n\n    // Short queries are OK (e.g., just a word or two)\n    let short_query = r#\"{\"message\": \"processing search\", \"q\": \"test\"}\"#;\n    assert!(!contains_pii(short_query), \"Short query should NOT be flagged as PII\");\n\n    // Long line without q= is OK\n    let long_safe = format!(r#\"{{\"message\": \"processing\", \"data\": \"{}\"}}\"#, \"x\".repeat(200));\n    assert!(!contains_pii(&long_safe), \"Long line without q= should NOT be flagged as PII\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_no_document_content_in_logs() {\n    // Large JSON blocks likely contain document content\n    let large_log = r#\"{\"message\": \"indexing document\", \"doc\": {\"#;\n    let large_log_padded = format!(\"{}{}\", large_log, \"x\".repeat(5000));\n documents\", \"count\": 100}\"#;\n    assert!(!contains_pii(normal_log), \"Normal log line should NOT be flagged\");\n}\n\n//     Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n\n    let headers = response.headers();\n    let request_id = headers.get(\"x-request-id\");\n    assert!(request_id.is_some(), \"X-Request-Id header should be present\");\n\n    let id_str = request_id.unwrap().to_str().unwrap();\n    assert!(\n        is_valid_request_id(id_str),\n        \"X-Request-Id should be 8 hex chars, got: {}\",\n        id_str\n    );\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_extension_accessible() {\n    // Acceptance criterion: Request.extensions().get::<RequestId>() works from any handler\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\"/test\", get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>| async move {\n            assert!(\n                is_valid_request_id(id.as_str()),\n                \"RequestId in extensions should be 8 hex chars\"\n            );\n            \"ok\"\n        }))\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_consecutive_requests_different_ids() {\n    // Acceptance criterion: Two consecutive requests produce different IDs\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::extract::State as AxumState;\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n    use std::sync::Arc;\n    use std::sync::Mutex;\n\n    let captured_ids = Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::<String>::new()));\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\n            \"/test\",\n            get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>, AxumState(ids): AxumState<Arc<Mutex<Vec<String>>>>| async move {\n                ids.lock().unwrap().push(id.as_str().to_string());\n                \"ok\"\n            }),\n        )\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware))\n        .with_state(captured_ids.clone());\n\n    // Make two requests consecutively\n    for _ in 0..2 {\n        let _ = app\n            .clone()\n            .oneshot(\n                Request::builder()\n                    .uri(\"/test\")\n                    .body(Body::empty())\n                    .unwrap(),\n            )\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n\n    let ids = captured_ids.lock().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(ids.len(), 2, \"Should have captured 2 request IDs\");\n    assert_ne!(ids[0], ids[1], \"Two consecutive requests should have different IDs\");\n    assert!(\n        is_valid_request_id(&ids[0]),\n        \"First ID should be 8 hex chars\"\n    );\n    assert!(\n        is_valid_request_id(&ids[1]),\n        \"Second ID should be 8 hex chars\"\n    );\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_preserves_existing_header() {\n    // If a request already has X-Request-Id, it should be preserved\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\n            \"/test\",\n            get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>| async move {\n                id.as_str().to_string()\n            }),\n        )\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    // Send a request with an existing X-Request-Id header\n    let existing_id = \"deadbeef\";\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .header(\"x-request-id\", existing_id)\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n\n    // The response should have the same X-Request-Id\n    let response_id = response.headers().get(\"x-request-id\").unwrap().to_str().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(response_id, existing_id, \"Should preserve existing X-Request-Id\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_invalid_header_is_replaced() {\n    // If a request has an invalid X-Request-Id (not 8 hex chars),\n    // the middleware should generate a new one\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\n            \"/test\",\n            get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>| async move {\n                id.as_str().to_string()\n            }),\n        )\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    // Send a request with an INVALID X-Request-Id header (wrong length)\n    let invalid_id = \"not-8-chars\";\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .header(\"x-request-id\", invalid_id)\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n\n    // The response should have a VALID 8-char hex ID (replaced)\n    let response_id = response.headers().get(\"x-request-id\").unwrap().to_str().unwrap();\n    assert_ne!(response_id, invalid_id, \"Should replace invalid X-Request-Id\");\n    assert!(\n        is_valid_request_id(response_id),\n        \"Replaced ID should be 8 hex chars\"\n    );\n}\n","numLines":498,"startLine":1,"totalLines":498}}}

%index))]\nasync fn search_handler(\n    Path(index): Path<String>,\n    Extension(state): Extension<Arc<AppState>>,\n    Json(body): Json<SearchRequestBody>,\n) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {\n    let start = Instant::now();\n    let client_requested_score = body.ranking_score.unwrap_or(false);\n\n    // Get the scoped key for this index (plan §13.21).\n    // If a scoped key exists, use primary_key (or previous_key during rotation overlap).\n    // If no scoped key exists yet, fall back to node_master_key for initial setup.\n    let search_key = if let Some(ref redis) = state.redis_store {\n        if let Ok(Some(sk)) = redis.get_search_ui_scoped_key(&index) {\n            // Refresh scoped-key beacon so the rotation leader knows this pod is serving\n            // requests for this index at the current generation (plan §13.21).\n            let _ = redis.observe_search_ui_scoped_key(\n                &state.pod_id,\n                &index,\n                sk.generation,\n            );\n\n            // Use primary_key; previous_key is the overlap fallback (both are valid in Meilisearch)\n            sk.primary_key\n        } else {\n            // No scoped key yet — fall back to node_master_key for initial setup\n            state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n        }\n    } else {\n        // No Redis store — fall back to node_master_key (single-pod dev mode)\n        state.config.node_master_key.clone()\n    };\n\n    // Use live topology from shared state (updated by health checker)\n    let topo = state.topology.read().await;\n    let policy = match state.config.scatter.unavailable_shard_policy.as_str() {\n        \"partial\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial,\n        \"error\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Error,\n        \"fallback\" => UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback,\n        _ => return Err(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR),\n    };\n\n    // Plan scatter using live topology (span for plan construction)\n    let plan = {\n        let _plan_span = tracing::info_span!(\n            \"scatter_plan\",\n            replica_groups = state.config.replica_groups,\n            shards = state.config.shards,\n            rf = state.config.replication_factor,\n        ).entered();\n        plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, state.config.replication_factor as usize, state.config.shards)\n    };\n    let node_count = plan.shard_to_node.len() as u64;\n\n    // Record scatter fan-out size before executing\n    state.metrics.record_scatter_fan_out(node_count);\n\n    // Build search request\n    let search_req = SearchRequest {\n        index_uid: index.clone(),\n        query: body.q,\n        offset: body.offset.unwrap_or(0),\n        limit: body.limit.unwrap_or(20),\n        filter: body.filter,\n        facets: body.facets,\n        ranking_score: client_requested_score,\n        body: body.rest,\n        global_idf: None,\n    };\n\n    // Create node client with the scoped key (or node_master_key as fallback)\n    let http_client = Arc::new(crate::client::HttpClient::new(\n        search_key,\n        state.config.scatter.node_timeout_ms,\n    ));\n    let client = ProxyNodeClient::new(http_client, state.metrics.clone());\n\n    // Use score-based merge strategy (OP#4: requires global IDF)\n    let strategy = ScoreMergeStrategy::new();\n\n    // Execute DFS query-then-fetch\n    let mut result = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(\n        plan,\n        &client,\n        search_req,\n        &topo,\n        policy,\n        &strategy,\n    )\n    .await\n    .map_err(|e| {\n        tracing::error!(error = %e, \"search failed\");\n        StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR\n    })?;\n\n    // Drop topology lock before building response\n    drop(topo);\n\n P7.5 Structured JSON logging + request IDs + trace correlation tests.\n//!\n//! Tests for plan §10 structured logging format:\n//! - JSON-per-line parseable by jq\n//! - request_id on every log line (same as X-Request-Id header)\n//! - No PII (API keys, document content, query strings) in logs\n//! - Log volume: 2 INFO entries per search request (middleware + search handler)\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::Request,\n    body::Body,\n    http::StatusCode,\n    Router,\n};\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\n\n/// Helper: parse a JSON log line and extract fields\nfn parse_log_line(line: &str) -> Option<serde_json::Value> {\n    serde_json::from_str(line).ok()\n}\n\n/// Helper: check if a string is a valid 8-char hex request ID (from RequestId::new)\nfn is_valid_request_id(s: &str) -> bool {\n    s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit())\n}\n\n/// Helper: check if a line contains PII patterns\nfn contains_pii(line: &str) -> bool {\n    let lower = line.to_lowercase();\n    // Check for API key patterns\n    if lower.contains(\"api_key\") || lower.contains(\"apikey\") || lower.contains(\"api-key\") {\n        // Allow if it's just \"api_key_hash\" or similar\n        if !lower.contains(\"hash\") && !lower.contains(\"uid\") {\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n    // Check for master keys\n    if lower.contains(\"master\") && (lower.contains(\"key\") || lower.contains(\"secret\")) {\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for JWT tokens\n    if lower.contains(\"eyj\") && lower.len() > 100 {\n        // JWT base64 pattern\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for document content patterns (long text blocks in logs)\n    if line.len() > 5000 {\n        // Very long log line might contain document content\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for raw query strings (both URL-style q= and JSON-style \"q\":)\n    if (lower.contains(\"q=\") || lower.contains(\"\\\"q\\\":\")) && line.len() > 200 {\n        // But not if the value is explicitly redacted\n        if !lower.contains(\"\\\"[redacted]\\\"\") {\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n    false\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance Criterion 1: jq parses every log line\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_json_logs_parseable_by_jq() {\n    // Sample log entry in the expected format\n    let sample_log = r#\"{\n        \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\n        \"level\": \"info\",\n        \"target\": \"miroir.request\",\n        \"message\": \"GET /indexes/products/search 200\",\n        \"request_id\": \"abc123\",\n        \"pod_id\": \"miroir-proxy-7f8d9c6d5-abc12\",\n        \"duration_ms\": 42,\n        \"status\": 200,\n        \"method\": \"GET\",\n        \"path_template\": \"/indexes/{uid}/search\"\n    }\"#;\n\n    let parsed = parse_log_line(sample_log);\n    assert!(parsed.is_some(), \"Sample log should parse as JSON\");\n\n    let log_obj = parsed.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"level\"], \"info\");\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"target\"], \"miroir.request\");\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"request_id\"], \"abc123\");\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"duration_ms\"], 42);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_request_id_format_in_logs() {\n    // Verify request_id appears in JSON logs\n    let sample_log = r#\"{\n        \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\n        \"level\": \"info\",\n        \"target\": \"miroir.request\",\n        \"message\": \"search completed\",\n        \"request_id\": \"a1b2c3d4\",\n        \"pod_id\": \"test-pod\",\n        \"duration_ms\": 42\n    }\"#;\n\n    let parsed = parse_log_line(sample_log).unwrap();\n    let request_id = parsed[\"request_id\"].as_str().unwrap();\n\n    // Request IDs should be 8 hex chars (from RequestId::new())\n    assert_eq!(request_id.len(), 8);\n    assert!(request_id.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()));\n}\n\n// r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.request\",\"request_id\":\"abc12345\",\"pod_id\":\"pod-1\",\"message\":\"GET /search 200\"}\"#,\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.001Z\",\"level\":\"debug\",\"target\":\"miroir.node\",\"request_id\":\"abc12345\",\"pod_id\":\"pod-1\",\"node_id\":\"node-1\",\"message\":\"node call started\"}\"#,\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.010Z\",\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.search\",\"request_id\":\"abc12345\",\"pod_id\":\"pod-1\",\"index\":\"products\",\"message\":\"search completed\"}\"#,\n    ];\n\n    // Extract all logs with request_id = \"abc12345\"\n    let target_id = \"abc12345\";\n    let matching_logs: Vec<_> = logs\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|line| line.contains(&format!(\"\\\"request_id\\\":\\\"{}\\\"\", target_id)))\n        .collect();\n\n    assert_eq!(matching_logs.len(), 3, \"Should find all 3 log lines for the request\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance Criterion 3: No PII in logs\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_no_api_keys_in_logs() {\n    // These patterns should NOT appear in logs\n    let pii_patterns = vec![\n        r#\"\"api_key\": \"master_key_12345\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"bearer\": \"Bearer master_key_12345\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"authorization\": \"Bearer master_key_12345\"\"#,\n    ];\n\n    for pattern in pii_patterns {\n        assert!(\n            contains_pii(pattern),\n            \"Pattern should be flagged as PII: {}\",\n            pattern\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Hashed versions are OK\n    let safe_patterns = vec![\n        r#\"\"api_key_hash\": \"a1b2c3d4\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"key_uid\": \"12345\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"tenant_id\": \"tenant-123\"\"#,\n    ];\n\n    for pattern in test_no_query_strings_in_logs() {\n    // Raw Meilisearch query content should not be logged\n    // The contains_pii function checks for long q= patterns (user queries)\n    // Line must be >200 chars total and contain \"q=\" to trigger PII detection\n    let padding = \"x\".repeat(200);\n    let bad_log = format!(r#\"{{\"message\": \"processing search\", \"q\": \"SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = 'test@example.com'\", \"padding\": \"{}\"}}\"#, padding);\n    assert!(contains_pii(&bad_log), \"Long query string should be flagged as PII\");\n\n    // Redacted queries are OK\n    let good_log = r#\"{\"message\": \"processing search\", \"query\": \"[redacted]\", \"q\": \"[redacted]\"}\"#;\n    assert!(!contains_pii(good_log), \"Redacted query should NOT be flagged as PII\");\n\n    // Short queries are OK (e.g., just a word or two)\n    let short_query = r#\"{\"message\": \"processing search\", \"q\": \"test\"}\"#;\n    assert!(!contains_pii(short_query), \"Short query should NOT be flagged as PII\");\n\n    // Long line without q= is OK\n    let long_safe = format!(r#\"{{\"message\": \"processing\", \"data\": \"{}\"}}\"#, \"x\".repeat(200));\n \"ok\"\n    }\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\"/test\", get(handler))\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n        );\n            \"ok\"\n        }))\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_consecutive_requests_different_ids() {\n    // Acceptance criterion: Two consecutive requests produce different IDs\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::extract::State as AxumState;\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n    use std::sync::Arc;\n    use std::sync::Mutex;\n\n    let captured_ids = Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::<String>::new()));\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\n            \"/test\",\n            get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>, AxumState(ids): AxumState<Arc<Mutex<Vec<String>>>>| async move {\n                ids.lock().unwrap().push(id.as_str().to_string());\n                \"ok\"\n            }),\n        )\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware))\n        .with_state(captured_ids.clone());\n\n    // Make two requests consecutively\n    for _ in 0..2 {\n        let _ = app\n            .clone()\n            .oneshot(\n                Request::builder()\n                    .uri(\"/test\")\n                    .body(Body::empty())\n                    .unwrap(),\n            )\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n\n    let ids = captured_ids.lock().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(ids.len(), 2, \"Should have captured 2 request IDs\");\n    assert_ne!(ids[0], ids[1], \"Two consecutive requests should have different IDs\");\n    assert!(\n        is_valid_request_id(&ids[0]),\n        \"First ID should be 8 hex chars\"\n    );\n    assert!(\n        is_valid_request_id(&ids[1]),\n        \"Second ID should be 8 hex chars\"\n    );\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_preserves_existing_header() {\n    // If a request already has X-Request-Id, it should be preserved\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\n            \"/test\",\n            get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>| async move {\n                id.as_str().to_string()\n            }),\n        )\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    // Send a request with an existing X-Request-Id header\n    let existing_id = \"deadbeef\";\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .header(\"x-request-id\", existing_id)\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n\n    // The response should have the same X-Request-Id\n    let response_id = response.headers().get(\"x-request-id\").unwrap().to_str().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(response_id, existing_id, \"Should preserve existing X-Request-Id\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_invalid_header_is_replaced() {\n    // If a request has an invalid X-Request-Id (not 8 hex chars),\n    // the middleware should generate a new one\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\n            \"/test\",\n            get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>| async move {\n                id.as_str().to_string()\n            }),\n        )\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    // Send a request with an INVALID X-Request-Id header (wrong length)\n    let invalid_id = \"not-8-chars\";\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .header(\"x-request-id\", invalid_id)\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n\n    // The response should have a VALID 8-char hex ID (replaced)\n    let response_id = response.headers().get(\"x-request-id\").unwrap().to_str().unwrap();\n    assert_ne!(response_id, invalid_id, \"Should replace invalid X-Request-Id\");\n    assert!(\n        is_valid_request_id(response_id),\n        \"Replaced ID should be 8 hex chars\"\n    );\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":495,"oldLines":3,"newStart":495,"newLines":137,"lines":[" tracing::info;","+    use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};","+","+    // Capture logs into a vector","+    let captured_logs = Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::new()));","+","+    // Create a custom layer that .with_current_span(true)  // This is the key: includes span fields on all events","+        .with_span_list(false)","+        .flatten_event(true)","+        .with_writer(std::sync::Mutex::new(","+            // Custom writer that captures JSON lines","+            struct CaptureWriter(Arc<Mutex<Vec<String>>>);","+            impl std::io::Write for CaptureWriter {","+                fn write(&mut self, buf: &[u8]) -> std::io::Result<usize> {","+                    if let Ok(s) = std::str::from_utf8(buf) {","+                        self.0.lock().unwrap().push(s.to_string());","+                    }","+                    Ok(buf.len())","+                }","+                fn flush(&mut self) -> std::io::Result<()> { Ok(()) }","+            }","+            CaptureWriter(logs_clone)","+        ));","+","+    // Initialize the subscriber with our capture layer","+    tracing_subscriber::registry()","+        .with(capture_layer)","+        .init();","+","+    // Create a handler that info!(message = \"handler completed\", step = \"3\");","+        \"ok\"","+    }","+","+    // Build telemetry state","+    let config = MiroirConfig::default();","+    let metrics = Request::builder()","+                .uri(\"/test\")","+                .body(Body::empty())","+                .unwrap(),","+        )","+        .await","+        .unwrap();","+","+ P7.5 Structured JSON logging + request IDs + trace correlation tests.\n//!\n//! Tests for plan §10 structured logging format:\n//! - JSON-per-line parseable by jq\n//! - request_id on every log line (same as X-Request-Id header)\n//! - No PII (API keys, document content, query strings) in logs\n//! - Log volume: 2 INFO entries per search request (middleware + search handler)\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::Request,\n    body::Body,\n    http::StatusCode,\n    Router,\n};\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\n\n/// Helper: parse a JSON log line and extract fields\nfn parse_log_line(line: &str) -> Option<serde_json::Value> {\n    serde_json::from_str(line).ok()\n}\n\n/// Helper: check if a string is a valid 8-char hex request ID (from RequestId::new)\nfn is_valid_request_id(s: &str) -> bool {\n    s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit())\n}\n\n/// Helper: check if a line contains PII patterns\nfn contains_pii(line: &str) -> bool {\n    let lower = line.to_lowercase();\n    // Check for API key patterns\n    if lower.contains(\"api_key\") || lower.contains(\"apikey\") || lower.contains(\"api-key\") {\n        // Allow if it's just \"api_key_hash\" or similar\n        if !lower.contains(\"hash\") && !lower.contains(\"uid\") {\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n    // Check for master keys\n    if lower.contains(\"master\") && (lower.contains(\"key\") || lower.contains(\"secret\")) {\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for JWT tokens\n    if lower.contains(\"eyj\") && lower.len() > 100 {\n        // JWT base64 pattern\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for document content patterns (long text blocks in logs)\n    if line.len() > 5000 {\n        // Very long log line might contain document content\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for raw query strings (both URL-style q= and JSON-style \"q\":)\n    if (lower.contains(\"q=\") || lower.contains(\"\\\"q\\\":\")) && line.len() > 200 {\n        // But not if the value is explicitly redacted\n        if !lower.contains(\"\\\"[redacted]\\\"\") {\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n    false\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance Extension};\n    use axum::extract::State as AxumState;\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n    use std::sync::Arc;\n    use     )\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware))\n        .with_state(captured_ids.clone());\n\n    // Make two requests consecutively\n    for _ in 0..2 {\n        let _ = app\n            .clone()\n            .oneshot(\n                Request::builder()\n                    .uri(\"/test\")\n                    .body(Body::empty())\n                    .unwrap(),\n            )\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n\n    let ids = captured_ids.lock().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(ids.len(), 2, \"Should have captured 2 request IDs\");\n    assert_ne!(ids[0], Router::new()\n        .route(\n            \"/test\",\n            get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>| async move {\n                id.as_str().to_string()\n            }),\n        )\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    // Send a request with an existing X-Request-Id header\n    let existing_id = \"deadbeef\";\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .header(\"x-request-id\", existing_id)\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n\n    // The response should have the same X-Request-Id\n    let response_id = test_request_id_invalid_header_is_replaced() {\n    // If a request has an invalid X-Request-Id (not 8 hex chars),\n    // the middleware should generate a new one\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\n            \"/test\",\n a request with an INVALID X-Request-Id header (wrong length)\n    let invalid_id = \"not-8-chars\";\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n is_valid_request_id(response_id),\n        \"Replaced ID should be 8 hex chars\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// P7.5.c Trace correlation: request_id appears in all log lines\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_appears_in_all_log_lines_within_request() {\n    // Acceptance criterion: Every log line inside a request must carry request_id=<id>\n    // This is achieved via tracing::Span with request_id recorded on span enter\n    // and tracing_subscriber::fmt().with_current_span(true)\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, telemetry_middleware, TelemetryState, RequestId, Metrics};\n    use miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\n    use tracing_subscriber::{layer::SubscriberExt, util::SubscriberInitExt};\n    use tracing::info;\n    use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\n\n    // Capture logs into a vector\n    let captured_logs = Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::new()));\n\n    // Create a custom layer that captures log events\n    let logs_clone = captured_logs.clone();\n    let capture_layer = tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer()\n        .json()\n        .with_target(true)\n        .with_current_span(true)  // This is the key: includes span fields on all events\n        .with_span_list(false)\n        .flatten_event(true)\n        .with_writer(std::sync::Mutex::new(\n            // Custom writer that captures JSON lines\n            struct CaptureWriter(Arc<Mutex<Vec<String>>>);\n            impl std::io::Write for CaptureWriter {\n                fn write(&mut self, buf: &[u8]) -> std::io::Result<usize> {\n                    if let Ok(s) = std::str::from_utf8(buf) {\n                        self.0.lock().unwrap().push(s.to_string());\n                    }\n                    Ok(buf.len())\n                }\n                fn flush(&mut self) -> std::io::Result<()> { Ok(()) }\n            }\n            CaptureWriter(logs_clone)\n        ));\n\n    // Initialize the subscriber with our capture layer\n    tracing_subscriber::registry()\n        .with(capture_layer)\n        .init();\n\n    // Create a handler that logs multiple times\n    async fn logging_handler(\n        Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>,\n        logs: Arc<Mutex<Vec<String>>>,\n    ) -> &'static str {\n        // These log events should all inherit the request_id from the parent span\n        info!(message = \"handler started\", step = \"1\");\n        info!(message = \"processing request\", step = \"2\");\n        info!(message = \"handler completed\", step = \"3\");\n        \"ok\"\n    }\n\n    // Build telemetry state\n    let config = MiroirConfig::default();\n    let metrics = P7.5 Structured JSON logging + request IDs + trace correlation tests.\n//!\n//! Tests for plan §10 structured logging format:\n//! - JSON-per-line parseable by jq\n//! - request_id on every log line (same as X-Request-Id header)\n//! - No PII (API keys, document content, query strings) in logs\n//! - Log volume: 2 INFO entries per search request (middleware + search handler)\n\nuse axum::{\n    extract::Request,\n    body::Body,\n    http::StatusCode,\n    Router,\n};\nuse tower::ServiceExt;\n\n/// Helper: parse a JSON log line and extract fields\nfn parse_log_line(line: &str) -> Option<serde_json::Value> {\n    serde_json::from_str(line).ok()\n}\n\n/// Helper: check if a string is a valid 8-char hex request ID (from RequestId::new)\nfn is_valid_request_id(s: &str) -> bool {\n    s.len() == 8 && s.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit())\n}\n\n/// Helper: check if a line contains PII patterns\nfn contains_pii(line: &str) -> bool {\n    let lower = line.to_lowercase();\n    // Check for API key patterns\n    if lower.contains(\"api_key\") || lower.contains(\"apikey\") || lower.contains(\"api-key\") {\n        // Allow if it's just \"api_key_hash\" or similar\n        if !lower.contains(\"hash\") && !lower.contains(\"uid\") {\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n    // Check for master keys\n    if lower.contains(\"master\") && (lower.contains(\"key\") || lower.contains(\"secret\")) {\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for JWT tokens\n    if lower.contains(\"eyj\") && lower.len() > 100 {\n        // JWT base64 pattern\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for document content patterns (long text blocks in logs)\n    if line.len() > 5000 {\n        // Very long log line might contain document content\n        return true;\n    }\n    // Check for raw query strings (both URL-style q= and JSON-style \"q\":)\n    if (lower.contains(\"q=\") || lower.contains(\"\\\"q\\\":\")) && line.len() > 200 {\n        // But not if the value is explicitly redacted\n        if !lower.contains(\"\\\"[redacted]\\\"\") {\n            return true;\n        }\n    }\n    false\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance Criterion 1: jq parses every log line\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_json_logs_parseable_by_jq() {\n    // Sample log entry in the expected format\n    let sample_log = r#\"{\n        \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\n        \"level\": \"info\",\n        \"target\": \"miroir.request\",\n        \"message\": \"GET /indexes/products/search 200\",\n        \"request_id\": \"abc123\",\n        \"pod_id\": \"miroir-proxy-7f8d9c6d5-abc12\",\n        \"duration_ms\": 42,\n        \"status\": 200,\n        \"method\": \"GET\",\n        \"path_template\": \"/indexes/{uid}/search\"\n    }\"#;\n\n    let parsed = parse_log_line(sample_log);\n    assert!(parsed.is_some(), \"Sample log should parse as JSON\");\n\n    let log_obj = parsed.unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"level\"], \"info\");\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"target\"], \"miroir.request\");\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"request_id\"], \"abc123\");\n    assert_eq!(log_obj[\"duration_ms\"], 42);\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_request_id_format_in_logs() {\n    // Verify request_id appears in JSON logs\n    let sample_log = r#\"{\n        \"timestamp\": \"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\n        \"level\": \"info\",\n        \"target\": \"miroir.request\",\n        \"message\": \"search completed\",\n        \"request_id\": \"a1b2c3d4\",\n        \"pod_id\": \"test-pod\",\n        \"duration_ms\": 42\n    }\"#;\n\n    let parsed = parse_log_line(sample_log).unwrap();\n    let request_id = parsed[\"request_id\"].as_str().unwrap();\n\n    // Request IDs should be 8 hex chars (from RequestId::new())\n    assert_eq!(request_id.len(), 8);\n    assert!(request_id.chars().all(|c| c.is_ascii_hexdigit()));\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_request_id_extraction_from_logs() {\n    let logs = vec![\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.000Z\",\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.request\",\"request_id\":\"abc12345\",\"pod_id\":\"pod-1\",\"message\":\"GET /search 200\"}\"#,\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.001Z\",\"level\":\"debug\",\"target\":\"miroir.node\",\"request_id\":\"abc12345\",\"pod_id\":\"pod-1\",\"node_id\":\"node-1\",\"message\":\"node call started\"}\"#,\n        r#\"{\"timestamp\":\"2026-05-01T12:00:00.010Z\",\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.search\",\"request_id\":\"abc12345\",\"pod_id\":\"pod-1\",\"index\":\"products\",\"message\":\"search completed\"}\"#,\n    ];\n\n    // Extract all logs with request_id = \"abc12345\"\n    let target_id = \"abc12345\";\n    let matching_logs: Vec<_> = logs\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|line| line.contains(&format!(\"\\\"request_id\\\":\\\"{}\\\"\", target_id)))\n        .collect();\n\n    assert_eq!(matching_logs.len(), 3, \"Should find all 3 log lines for the request\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Acceptance Criterion 3: No PII in logs\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_no_api_keys_in_logs() {\n    // These patterns should NOT appear in logs\n    let pii_patterns = vec![\n        r#\"\"api_key\": \"master_key_12345\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"bearer\": \"Bearer master_key_12345\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"authorization\": \"Bearer master_key_12345\"\"#,\n    ];\n\n    for pattern in pii_patterns {\n        assert!(\n            contains_pii(pattern),\n            \"Pattern should be flagged as PII: {}\",\n            pattern\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Hashed versions are OK\n    let safe_patterns = vec![\n        r#\"\"api_key_hash\": \"a1b2c3d4\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"key_uid\": \"12345\"\"#,\n        r#\"\"tenant_id\": \"tenant-123\"\"#,\n    ];\n\n    for pattern in test_no_query_strings_in_logs() {\n    // Raw Meilisearch query content should not be logged\n    // The contains_pii function checks for long q= patterns (user queries)\n    // Line must be >200 chars total and contain \"q=\" to trigger PII detection\n    let padding = \"x\".repeat(200);\n    let bad_log = format!(r#\"{{\"message\": \"processing search\", \"q\": \"SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = 'test@example.com'\", \"padding\": \"{}\"}}\"#, padding);\n    assert!(contains_pii(&bad_log), \"Long query string should be flagged as PII\");\n\n    // Redacted queries are OK\n    let good_log = r#\"{\"message\": \"processing search\", \"query\": \"[redacted]\", \"q\": \"[redacted]\"}\"#;\n    assert!(!contains_pii(good_log), \"Redacted query should NOT be flagged as PII\");\n\n    // Short queries are OK (e.g., just a word or two)\n    let short_query = r#\"{\"message\": \"processing search\", \"q\": \"test\"}\"#;\n    assert!(!contains_pii(short_query), \"Short query should NOT be flagged as PII\");\n\n    // Long line without q= is OK\n    let long_safe = format!(r#\"{{\"message\": \"processing\", \"data\": \"{}\"}}\"#, \"x\".repeat(200));\n    assert!(!contains_pii(&long_safe), \"Long line without q= should NOT be flagged as PII\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_no_document_content_in_logs() {\n    // Large JSON blocks likely contain document ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[test]\nfn test_middleware_log_format() {\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{generate_request_id, RequestIdExt};\n    use axum::http::HeaderMap;\n\n    // Test request_id generation format\n    let id = generate_request_id();\n    assert_eq!(id.len(), 16, \"Request ID should be 16 hex chars\");\n (r#\"{\"level\":\"warn\",\"target\":\"miroir.request\",\"message\":\"degraded response\"}\"#, \"warn\"),\n        (r#\"{\"level\":\"info\",\"target\":\"miroir.request\",\"message\":\"GET / 200\"}\"#, (r#\"{\"level\":\"trace\",\"target\":\"miroir.scatter\",\"message\":\"fan-out buffer\"}\"#, \"trace\"),\n    ];\n\n    for (log, expected_level) in logs_by_level {\n        let parsed = ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_response_header() {\n    // Acceptance criterion: Every response includes X-Request-Id: <8-char hex>\n    use axum::routing::get;\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::request_id_middleware;\n\n    async fn handler() -> &'static str {\n        \"ok\"\n    }\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\"/test\", get(handler))\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n\n    let headers = response.headers();\n    let request_id = headers.get(\"x-request-id\");\n    assert!(request_id.is_some(), \"X-Request-Id header should be present\");\n\n    let id_str = request_id.unwrap().to_str().unwrap();\n    assert!(\n        is_valid_request_id(id_str),\n        \"X-Request-Id should be 8 hex chars, got: {}\",\n        id_str\n    );\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_extension_accessible() {\n    // Acceptance criterion: Request.extensions().get::<RequestId>() works from any handler\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\"/test\", get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>| async move {\n            assert!(\n                is_valid_request_id(id.as_str()),\n                \"RequestId in extensions should be 8 hex chars\"\n            );\n            \"ok\"\n        }))\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_consecutive_requests_different_ids() {\n    // Acceptance criterion: Two consecutive requests produce different IDs\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::extract::State as AxumState;\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n    use std::sync::Arc;\n    use std::sync::Mutex;\n\n    let captured_ids = Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::<String>::new()));\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\n            \"/test\",\n            get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>, AxumState(ids): AxumState<Arc<Mutex<Vec<String>>>>| async move {\n                ids.lock().unwrap().push(id.as_str().to_string());\n                \"ok\"\n            }),\n        )\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware))\n        .with_state(captured_ids.clone());\n\n    // Make two requests consecutively\n    for _ in 0..2 {\n        let _ = app\n            .clone()\n            .oneshot(\n                Request::builder()\n                    .uri(\"/test\")\n                    .body(Body::empty())\n                    .unwrap(),\n            )\n            .await\n            .unwrap();\n    }\n\n    let ids = captured_ids.lock().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(ids.len(), 2, \"Should have captured 2 request IDs\");\n    assert_ne!(ids[0], ids[1], \"Two consecutive requests should have different IDs\");\n    assert!(\n        is_valid_request_id(&ids[0]),\n        \"First ID should be 8 hex chars\"\n    );\n    assert!(\n        is_valid_request_id(&ids[1]),\n        \"Second ID should be 8 hex chars\"\n    );\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_preserves_existing_header() {\n    // If a request already has X-Request-Id, it should be preserved\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\n            \"/test\",\n            get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>| async move {\n                id.as_str().to_string()\n            }),\n        )\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    // Send a request with an existing X-Request-Id header\n    let existing_id = \"deadbeef\";\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .header(\"x-request-id\", existing_id)\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n\n    // The response should have the same X-Request-Id\n    let response_id = response.headers().get(\"x-request-id\").unwrap().to_str().unwrap();\n    assert_eq!(response_id, existing_id, \"Should preserve existing X-Request-Id\");\n}\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_invalid_header_is_replaced() {\n    // If a request has an invalid X-Request-Id (not 8 hex chars),\n    // the middleware should generate a new one\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, RequestId};\n\n    let app = Router::new()\n        .route(\n            \"/test\",\n            get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>| async move {\n                id.as_str().to_string()\n            }),\n        )\n        .layer(middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    // Send a request with an INVALID X-Request-Id header (wrong length)\n    let invalid_id = \"not-8-chars\";\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .header(\"x-request-id\", invalid_id)\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n\n    // The response should have a VALID 8-char hex ID (replaced)\n    let response_id = response.headers().get(\"x-request-id\").unwrap().to_str().unwrap();\n    assert_ne!(response_id, invalid_id, \"Should replace invalid X-Request-Id\");\n    assert!(\n        is_valid_request_id(response_id),\n        \"Replaced ID should be 8 hex chars\"\n    );\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// P7.5.c Trace correlation: request_id appears in all log lines\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n#[tokio::test]\nasync fn test_request_id_appears_in_all_log_lines_within_request() {\n    // Acceptance criterion: Every log line inside a request must carry request_id=<id>\n    // This is achieved via tracing::Span with request_id recorded on span enter\n    // and tracing_subscriber::fmt().with_current_span(true)\n    use axum::{routing::get, Extension};\n    use axum::middleware;\n    use miroir_proxy::middleware::{request_id_middleware, telemetry_middleware, TelemetryState, RequestId, Metrics};\n    use miroir_core::config::MiroirConfig;\n    use tracing_subscriber::{layer::SubscriberExt, util::SubscriberInitExt};\n    use tracing::info;\n    use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\n\n    // Capture logs into a vector\n    let captured_logs = Arc::new(Mutex::new(Vec::<String>::new()));\n\n    // Create a custom layer that captures log events\n    let logs_clone = captured_logs.clone();\n    let capture_layer = tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer()\n        .json()\n        .with_target(true)\n        .with_current_span(true)  // This is the key: includes span fields on all events\n        .with_span_list(false)\n        .flatten_event(true)\n        .with_writer(std::sync::Mutex::new(\n            // Custom writer that captures JSON lines\n            struct CaptureWriter(Arc<Mutex<Vec<String>>>);\n            impl std::io::Write for CaptureWriter {\n                fn write(&mut self, buf: &[u8]) -> std::io::Result<usize> {\n                    if let Ok(s) = std::str::from_utf8(buf) {\n                        self.0.lock().unwrap().push(s.to_string());\n                    }\n                    Ok(buf.len())\n                }\n                fn flush(&mut self) -> std::io::Result<()> { Ok(()) }\n            }\n            CaptureWriter(logs_clone)\n        ));\n\n    // Initialize the subscriber with our capture layer\n    tracing_subscriber::registry()\n        .with(capture_layer)\n        .init();\n\n    // Build telemetry state\n    let config = MiroirConfig::default();\n    let metrics = Metrics::new(&config);\n    let telemetry = TelemetryState {\n        metrics: metrics.clone(),\n        pod_id: \"test-pod\".to_string(),\n    };\n\n    // Build app with full middleware stack\n    let app = axum::Router::new()\n        .route(\"/test\", get(|Extension(id): Extension<RequestId>| async move {\n            // These log events should all inherit the request_id from the parent span\n            info!(message = \"handler started\", step = \"1\");\n            info!(message = \"processing request\", step = \"2\");\n            info!(message = \"handler completed\", step = \"3\");\n            \"ok\"\n        }))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn_with_state(\n            telemetry.clone(),\n            telemetry_middleware,\n        ))\n        .layer(axum::middleware::from_fn(request_id_middleware));\n\n    // Make a request\n    let response = app\n        .oneshot(\n            Request::builder()\n                .uri(\"/test\")\n                .body(Body::empty())\n                .unwrap(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .unwrap();\n\n    assert_eq!(response.status(), StatusCode::OK);\n\n    // Extract the request_id from the response header\n    let request_id = response.headers()\n        .get(\"x-request-id\")\n        .unwrap()\n        .to_str()\n        .unwrap();\n\n    // Verify all captured log lines contain the request_id\n    let logs = captured_logs.lock().unwrap();\n    let log_lines: Vec<&str> = logs.iter()\n        .flat_map(|s| s.lines())\n        .filter(|line| line.contains(\"message\"))  // Only look at actual log lines\n        .collect();\n\n    // We should have at least 3 log lines from the handler\n    assert!(\n        log_lines.len() >= 3,\n        \"Expected at least 3 log lines from handler, got: {}\",\n        log_lines.len()\n    );\n\n    // Every log line should contain the request_id\n    for line in &log_lines {\n        let json = parse_log_line(line).expect(&format!(\"Log line should be valid JSON: {}\", line));\n\n        // Verify request_id field exists and matches the response header\n        assert!(\n            json.get(\"request_id\").is_some(),\n            \"Log line missing request_id field: {}\",\n            line\n        );\n\n        let log_request_id = json[\"request_id\"].as_str().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            log_request_id, request_id,\n            \"Log line request_id should match response header: {}\",\n            line\n        );\n    }\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":500,"oldLines":6,"newStart":500,"newLines":24,"lines":[" // P7.5.c Trace correlation: request_id appears in all log lines"," //
